recognized_artifact: Scrum Sprint / iterative time-boxed development cycle (e.g., Scrum, Kanban sprint, or similar agile iteration)

entry_conditions.value: A near-term working target must be defined before the cycle begins
entry_conditions.ambiguity: The scope or granularity of 'near-term' is unspecified; could range from hours to weeks

entry_conditions.value: Available work must exist and be prioritized prior to commitment
entry_conditions.ambiguity: Whether prioritization is a precondition to the cycle or the first step within it is ambiguous

exit_conditions.value: The cycle produces an immediately usable result
exit_conditions.source_text: Carry out the work to produce an immediately usable result.
exit_conditions.ambiguity: 'Immediately usable' is undefined; could mean shippable, demonstrable, or merely functional

exit_conditions.value: Reflection on the cycle outcome is completed
exit_conditions.source_text: Reflect on the cycle outcome and reassess the next cycle.
exit_conditions.ambiguity: Whether reflection is an exit condition or a transition step into the next cycle is unclear

required_steps.ambiguity: Prioritization method or criteria are not specified

required_steps.value: Commit to work to be taken into the current cycle
required_steps.source_text: Commit to what will be taken into the current work cycle.
required_steps.ambiguity: Whether 'commit' implies a formal agreement or informal selection is unspecified

required_steps.value: Execute the work to produce an immediately usable result
required_steps.source_text: Carry out the work to produce an immediately usable result.
required_steps.ambiguity: The nature of the work and the definition of 'usable' are unspecified

required_steps.value: Reflect on the cycle outcome and reassess the next cycle
required_steps.source_text: Reflect on the cycle outcome and reassess the next cycle.
required_steps.ambiguity: Whether 'reassess' feeds back into target definition or prioritization is ambiguous

loops.value: The full five-step sequence repeats; each cycle ends with reassessment that initiates the next cycle
loops.source_text: Reflect on the cycle outcome and reassess the next cycle.
loops.ambiguity: The packet does not explicitly state the loop repeats indefinitely; it could describe a single cycle

branches.value: Reassessment may alter the target or priority ordering for the next cycle
branches.source_text: Reflect on the cycle outcome and reassess the next cycle.
branches.ambiguity: The packet does not specify what outcomes of reassessment are possible or how they affect subsequent steps

termination_conditions.value: No explicit termination condition is stated; the loop could be indefinite or externally terminated
termination_conditions.ambiguity: The packet describes a repeating cycle with no stated end; termination may be implicit in context not provided

structural_layer.control_flow_shape: Linear sequence within each cycle, with a closing reflective step that strongly implies a loop back to the first step. Overall shape: iterative loop (plan → prioritize → commit → execute → reflect → repeat).

primitive_sequence: SELECT / COMMIT (work items for current cycle)
transitions: DEFINE → PRIORITIZE: target scopes the prioritization
PRIORITIZE → SELECT/COMMIT: ordered work enables commitment decision
SELECT/COMMIT → EXECUTE: committed scope gates execution
EVALUATE → REASSESS: reflection informs next-cycle planning
REASSESS → DEFINE (loop): reassessment initiates the next cycle
primitive_layer.dominant_primitive: EXECUTE (the carrying-out step is the primary value-producing primitive)
candidate_missing_primitives: REVIEW / DEMO (sharing the usable result with stakeholders — not mentioned but commonly associated)
RETROSPECT (team-level process reflection distinct from outcome reflection — not distinguishable from packet alone)

invariants.value: Each cycle must produce an immediately usable result
invariants.source_text: Carry out the work to produce an immediately usable result.
invariants.ambiguity: 'Immediately usable' is not defined; its invariance across all cycles is inferred

invariants.value: Work committed must come from the prioritized pool of available work
invariants.source_text: Prioritize available work. Commit to what will be taken into the current work cycle.
invariants.ambiguity: Whether ad-hoc additions to the committed set are prohibited is not stated

preconditions.value: Available work must exist before prioritization can occur
preconditions.ambiguity: The source or composition of available work is not described

postconditions.value: An immediately usable result exists at cycle end
postconditions.source_text: Carry out the work to produce an immediately usable result.

postconditions.value: The next cycle has been reassessed before the current cycle closes
postconditions.source_text: Reflect on the cycle outcome and reassess the next cycle.
postconditions.ambiguity: The depth or formality of reassessment is unspecified

stopping_criteria.value: No explicit stopping criterion present in the packet
stopping_criteria.ambiguity: Termination may be externally imposed or implied by context outside this packet

validity_conditions.value: A cycle is valid only if it produces an immediately usable result
validity_conditions.source_text: Carry out the work to produce an immediately usable result.
validity_conditions.ambiguity: Whether a cycle with a partial result is considered valid is not addressed

representation_layer.natural_language_summary: A repeating work cycle in which a near-term target is first defined, available work is prioritized, a subset of that work is committed to the current cycle, the work is executed until an immediately usable result is produced, and the cycle closes with reflection on the outcome and reassessment of the next cycle. The reassessment step feeds back into the start of the next cycle, creating an iterative loop of indefinite length.
representation_layer.canonical_summary: DEFINE target → PRIORITIZE work → COMMIT scope → EXECUTE to usable result → REFLECT and REASSESS → repeat

ambiguities.description: Whether 'Define a near-term working target' and 'Prioritize available work' are strictly sequential or partially concurrent is not specified
plausible_interpretations: Target definition scopes and precedes prioritization
Prioritization and target definition are co-determined in a single planning activity
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Minor; sequence ordering of first two primitives may vary

ambiguities.description: The packet contains no explicit termination condition for the outer loop
plausible_interpretations: The cycle repeats indefinitely until externally stopped
The cycle has an implied termination tied to a larger goal not described in this packet
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Stopping criteria field cannot be populated from packet content alone

ambiguities.description: 'Reflect on the cycle outcome' and 'reassess the next cycle' may be one activity or two distinct activities
plausible_interpretations: A single retrospective activity combining outcome review and next-cycle planning
Two separate activities: a backward-looking retrospective and a forward-looking planning update
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Affects whether the final step maps to one primitive or two

ambiguities.description: Whether reflection and reassessment are the final step of the current cycle or the first step of the next is ambiguous
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Affects placement of these primitives relative to the loop boundary

clarity_relevance_observations.value: The procedure is compact and communicable; each step maps to a single clear imperative, which supports clarity in tooling that must surface or guide iterative work
clarity_relevance_observations.source_text: Define a near-term working target. Prioritize available work. Commit to what will be taken into the current work cycle. Carry out the work to produce an immediately usable result. Reflect on the cycle outcome and reassess the next cycle.
clarity_relevance_observations.ambiguity: Relevance to a specific 'Clarity' product is not inferable from the packet alone

edf_relevance_observations.value: The commitment step introduces a formal scope gate between prioritization and execution, which is structurally relevant to any execution-decision framework that models staged commitment
edf_relevance_observations.source_text: Commit to what will be taken into the current work cycle.
edf_relevance_observations.ambiguity: EDF interpretation is speculative without additional product context

research_only_observations.value: The procedure's five-step form with embedded loop is a minimal iterative cycle; its compactness makes it a useful calibration artifact for procedural extraction studies testing loop detection and primitive sequencing
research_only_observations.ambiguity: Research relevance is assessed from the study context, not the packet content

confidence.structural_layer: high — all five steps are explicit; loop is strongly implied; branches and termination are absent or unspecified
confidence.primitive_layer: high for sequence identification; moderate for transition semantics and loop boundary placement
confidence.constraint_layer: moderate — preconditions and postconditions are derivable; stopping criteria and validity edge cases are underspecified
confidence.representation_layer: high for summary and AST structure; moderate for ambiguity resolution
confidence.product_relevance_layer: low — product context (Clarity, EDF) is not described in the packet; observations are speculative
confidence.overall: moderate-high — the procedural content is clear and fully explicit; uncertainty is concentrated in loop termination, step boundaries, and product relevance

notes: The blinded procedural content maps cleanly onto a five-step iterative work cycle. The recognized artifact is noted in the recognized_artifact field only. No steps have been added based on recognition; the optional_steps array is empty because the packet supports no optional steps. The candidate_missing_primitives in the primitive layer record commonly associated steps (demo, retrospect) that are absent from the packet and have not been introduced into the extraction.
